easy.question: What is the capital of Australia?
easy.comment: Canberra was purpose-built as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne, both of which wanted to be the capital.
easy.taunt: Oh no, you failed 5th grade geography. Womp womp.

easy.question: Which country has the most natural lakes?
easy.comment: Canada has over 60% of the world's lakes — more than all other countries combined.
easy.taunt: It's literally the country shaped like a lake magnet. Come on.

medium.question: What is the smallest country in the world by area?
medium.comment: Vatican City covers just 0.44 km² and is entirely surrounded by Rome.
medium.taunt: It's been in every trivia game since the beginning of time. No excuses.

hard.question: Which African country was formerly known as Dahomey?
hard.comment: Dahomey was renamed Benin in 1975, after the Bight of Benin on its southern coast.
hard.taunt: Okay, that one was genuinely hard. We'll let this slide.
